title: Wall Street’s AI divide: Inside the bull and bear cases for what comes next for the stock market
excerpt: Three years into the AI trade, Wall Street is no closer to agreeing about whether it’s a bubble, how much longer it can keep going or what might ultimately e...
content_html:
Three years into the artificial-intelligence trade, the U.S. market has delivered some of the best returns, and the most extreme concentration, in decades. Yet Wall Street is no closer to agreeing about whether it's a bubble, how much longer it can keep going or what might ultimately end it.

That divide was on full display in a recent internal debate among BCA Research's top macroeconomic experts. A closer look at the discussion shows just how far apart different views remain, and why it's a debate every investor is taking part in whether they realize it or not.

Most Read from MarketWatch

  • I will definitely claim Social Security early. Why do so few people talk about the elephant in the room?

  • The co-founder of Stocktwits dumped chip stocks before their 20% slide. Where he's putting his money now.

  • Do I use $300,000 of my $1.2 million retirement savings so my daughter can attend her dream college?

Peter Berezin, chief economist at BCA Research, and Arthur Budaghyan, head of core macroeconomics and chief emerging-markets strategist, said their bearish view rests on the idea that the U.S. stock market is in the midst of an earnings bubble.

"Although profits are certainly quite strong, margins are less durable than they appear, and valuations have already priced in a best-case outcome with little room left for surprise," Berezin and Budaghyan said during a bulls vs. bears debate webcast last week.

Stock valuations look stretched by almost every measure, they said. Adjusted back to 2019 profit margins, the S&P 500 SPX would trade at 27 times forward earnings today, above the peak of roughly 26.5 times reached in March 2000.

Stripping out technology XX:SP500.45 and financial stocks XX:SP500.40, the rest of the stock market is not cheap either, trading at 26 times trailing earnings with only about 3% profit growth over the past two to three years, according to data compiled by BCA Research (see charts below).

See: Is an opportunity to buy chip stocks nearing? These two big Wall Street banks are divided.

In terms of the massive capital expenditures in AI — with Big Tech hyperscalers collectively pouring hundreds of billions of dollars into data centers, specialized chips, power grids and energy upgrades — Berezin and Budaghyan said the huge amount of capex is generating earnings but eroding cash flow.

"Every chip sold registers as revenue and profit for the seller, and the hyperscalers buying those chips treat the purchase as capex rather than an operating cost, so reported earnings rise without a matching increase in cash flow," they said.

sentences.zh: 根据 BCA Research 编制的数据（见下图），除去科技股 XX:SP500.45 和金融股 XX:SP500.40，股市的其余部分也不便宜，其交易市盈率为 26 倍，过去两到三年的利润增长率仅为 3% 左右。

sentences.en: These two big Wall Street banks are divided.
sentences.zh: 这两家华尔街大银行存在分歧。

blocks.html_en: In terms of the massive capital expenditures in AI — with Big Tech hyperscalers collectively pouring hundreds of billions of dollars into data centers, specialized chips, power grids and energy upgrades — Berezin and Budaghyan said the huge amount of capex is generating earnings but eroding cash flow.

sentences.en: In terms of the massive capital expenditures in AI — with Big Tech hyperscalers collectively pouring hundreds of billions of dollars into data centers, specialized chips, power grids and energy upgrades — Berezin and Budaghyan said the huge amount of capex is generating earnings but eroding cash flow.
sentences.zh: 就人工智能领域的巨额资本支出而言，大型科技超大规模企业总共向数据中心、专用芯片、电网和能源升级投入了数千亿美元，Berezin 和 Budaghyan 表示，巨额资本支出正在产生收益，但侵蚀了现金流。

blocks.html_en: "Every chip sold registers as revenue and profit for the seller, and the hyperscalers buying those chips treat the purchase as capex rather than an operating cost, so reported earnings rise without a matching increase in cash flow," they said.

sentences.en: "Every chip sold registers as revenue and profit for the seller, and the hyperscalers buying those chips treat the purchase as capex rather than an operating cost, so reported earnings rise without a matching increase in cash flow," they said.
sentences.zh: 他们表示：“售出的每块芯片都被视为卖方的收入和利润，而购买这些芯片的超大规模企业将购买视为资本支出而不是运营成本，因此报告的盈利增加了，但现金流却没有相应增加。”

content_html_zh:
人工智能贸易三年来，美国市场带来了几十年来最好的回报和最极端的集中度。 然而，对于泡沫是否是泡沫、泡沫还能持续多久以及最终可能会以何种方式结束泡沫，华尔街尚未达成共识。

这种分歧在 BCA Research 顶级宏观经济专家最近的内部辩论中得到了充分体现。 仔细观察这场讨论就会发现，不同的观点仍然存在多大的分歧，以及为什么每个投资者都在参与这场辩论，无论他们是否意识到这一点。

大多数阅读来自 MarketWatch

  • 我一定会尽早申请社会保障。 为什么很少有人谈论房间里的大象？
  • Stocktwits 联合创始人在芯片股下跌 20% 之前抛售了该股。 他现在把钱放在哪里。
  • 我是否可以使用 120 万美元退休储蓄中的 30 万美元让我的女儿能够进入她梦想的大学？

BCA Research首席经济学家Peter Berezin和核心宏观经济主管兼首席新兴市场策略师Arthur Budaghyan表示，他们的看跌观点基于美国股市正处于盈利泡沫之中的观点。

贝雷津和布达吉安在上周的多头与空头辩论网络直播中表示，“尽管利润肯定相当强劲，但利润率并不像看上去那么持久，而且估值已经反映了最好情况的结果，几乎没有什么惊喜的空间。”

他们表示，从几乎所有指标来看，股票估值似乎都过高。 调整回 2019 年的利润率后，标准普尔 500 SPX 指数今天的预期市盈率为 27 倍，高于 2000 年 3 月达到的约 26.5 倍的峰值。

根据 BCA Research 编制的数据（见下图），除去科技股 XX:SP500.45 和金融股 XX:SP500.40，股市的其余部分也不便宜，其交易市盈率为 26 倍，过去两到三年的利润增长率仅为 3% 左右。

看看：买入芯片股的机会即将来临吗？ 这两家华尔街大银行存在分歧。

就人工智能领域的巨额资本支出而言，大型科技超大规模企业总共向数据中心、专用芯片、电网和能源升级投入了数千亿美元，Berezin 和 Budaghyan 表示，巨额资本支出正在产生收益，但侵蚀了现金流。

他们表示：“售出的每块芯片都被视为卖方的收入和利润，而购买这些芯片的超大规模企业将购买视为资本支出而不是运营成本，因此报告的盈利增加了，但现金流却没有相应增加。”
